internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} (capability : CT2.Capability P Target)
  (ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target) (input : CT2.Input capability ctx),
  match CT2.analyzeDeletion capability ctx input with
  | CT2.DeletionDecision.closes witness =>
    P.Baseline (capability.reductions.delete input.seed.piece).value ∧
      ¬Target (capability.reductions.delete input.seed.piece).value
  | CT2.DeletionDecision.critical state =>
    ¬(P.Baseline (capability.reductions.delete input.seed.piece).value ∧
        ¬Target (capability.reductions.delete input.seed.piece).value)
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.analyzeDeletion_sound
internalDeclarations.docString: Both deletion branches carry their exact mathematical dichotomy.

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} (ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target)
  (smaller : Core.SmallerObject P ctx.G), P.Baseline smaller.value → ¬Target smaller.value → False
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.SmallerObject.counterexample_impossible
internalDeclarations.docString: A smaller target-avoiding baseline object contradicts minimality.
